hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W, F, Y) will enhance complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/TM5 residues (e.g., E192, K205), while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L, I, V) will improve steric fit in the SSTR2-unique TM6 pocket (272/273), thereby increasing selectivity margin over off-targets SSTR3/SSTR5.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current designs are achieving strong SSTR2 binding but are not generating sufficient diversity in the non-pharmacophore regions to disrupt off-target interactions. Targeted mutagenesis at positions 1/2/5/6/11/12 is essential to improve selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target binding (particularly with SSTR3/SSTR5). Focusing mutations here can disrupt off-target interactions without compromising the core pharmacophore.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved pocket interactions in SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the native pharmacophore (FWKT at positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
